title: Pronunciation — Czech Speaker Traps
topics: TH sound · W vs V · vowels · word stress · silent letters · rhythm
description: Nejčastější výslovnostní chyby Čechů v angličtině.

vocabulary.cz: člen THE — před souhláskou /ðə/, před samohláskou /ði/
velmi — NE 'wery', V = zuby na ret
šva — nejčastější zvuk angličtiny: about, the, a

notes: Voiced /ð/: the, this, that, they, brother — tongue between teeth, buzz
Voiceless /θ/: think, three, thank, mouth — tongue between teeth, no buzz
W vs V: W = rounded lips, blow air | V = top teeth on lower lip
Short vs long vowels: ship/sheep | live/leave | pull/pool
Silent letters: know, write, knife, Wednesday, doubt, could
Schwa /ə/ — the most common English sound: 'a-BOUT', 'the', 'to-DAY'

examples.cz: Jazyk musí vyjít mezi zuby pro TH
W = kulatá ústa | V = zuby na ret

examples.en: I live /lɪv/ in Prague. I leave /liːv/ at 8.

quiz.sentence: 'Three' rhymes with: tree, free, or zee?
The silent letter in 'knife' is: k, n, or e?
'Ship' has a ___ vowel; 'sheep' has a ___ vowel.

tasks: 1. Record yourself reading the first 10 words from the TH section
2. Find 5 words you regularly mispronounce — look them up in a dictionary
3. Read the dialogue from Lesson 15 aloud, focusing on TH and W/V sounds
